pointsAdjustments.reason: Austrian GP: missing ECU seal; Hakkinen kept the win, the team was stripped of the constructors points.

setupGuide.notes: Placeholder for Melbourne Grand Prix Circuit - 58 laps / 307.6 km reproduced as 96 laps of Adelaide Modern. Modern Adelaide keeps the Australian parkland-circuit character.
No 2000 Interlagos layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-close 1991 layout.

track.realVenue: Autodromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari

setupGuide.notes: No 2000 Imola in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-close 2001 layout.
No 2000 Silverstone in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the near-identical 2001 layout.
No 2000 Barcelona layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-matching 1999 layout.
The 2000 European GP ran the Nürburgring GP-Strecke before the 2002 Mercedes-Arena extension; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the modern GP layout.
2000 Monaco predates the 2003-04 harbour-front reprofiles, so the 1988 layout is the era-closest; the venue is real and the historical lap count is kept.
2000 Montreal is the same venue as Montreal Modern, so the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours - 72 laps / 306 km reproduced as 73 laps of Le Mans Circuit Bugatti. Keeps the French GP at a French Grand Prix venue (the curated 1991+ era override avoids double-booking this season's Spanish GP at Barcelona).
The 2000 Austrian GP ran the A1-Ring; AMS2's Spielberg 2001 is that same layout (f1db lists the venue under its current Red Bull Ring name), so the historical lap count is kept.
No 2000 Hockenheim in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the near-identical 2001 layout of the pre-2002 forest circuit.
AMS2 ships the 2025 Hungaroring; the venue is real and largely unchanged in character since 1986, so the historical lap count is kept.
No 2000 Spa layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-closest 1993 layout.
No 2000 Monza in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-matching 1991 layout (pre-2000 first-chicane configuration).
The inaugural 2000 US GP ran the Indianapolis road course; AMS2's Indianapolis 2001 road course is that same layout, so the historical lap count is kept.
Suzuka appears in AMS2 as Kansai; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the modern GP layout.

Placeholder for Sepang International Circuit - 56 laps / 310.4 km reproduced as 68 laps of the modern Hockenheim GP layout. Both are Hermann Tilke designs of the same era; the redesigned Hockenheim GP keeps Sepang's long-straight-into-tight-hairpin rhythm and wide modern-GP character.
